title: Reload friction
principles: Reload friction describes how magazine recovery delays or interrupts continued operation of an attack mode.
Recovery model and friction severity must remain separate because full-magazine, incremental, regenerative, and staged systems can each produce different friction levels.
Recovery delay, partial recovery time, full recovery time, and recurrence frequency are distinct properties and must not be collapsed into one reload duration.
Active magazine duration may be estimated only when the evaluated mode has a defined and sufficiently stable magazine-consumption rate.
A standardized full-depletion cycle may support comparison, but it does not represent guaranteed practical use.
Reload friction must be evaluated separately for modes with different magazine costs, consumption patterns, or recovery models.
Comparisons between recovery models must preserve their distinct timing and interruption behavior.

low: Mandatory recovery costs are limited relative to the supported active-operation scenario.
Neither interruption duration nor recurrence clearly dominates the evaluated cycle.
moderate: Magazine recovery creates noticeable friction without consistently dominating the supported operating cycle.
The explanation must identify whether the main cost is delay, duration, recurrence, or incomplete recovery.
high: Magazine recovery creates substantial or frequently recurring loss of active operation.
The dominant recovery cost must remain explicitly identified.
undetermined: The supplied consumption rate, recovery model, or required timing fields are insufficient to classify reload friction.

full_magazine_recovery: Use reload_time as the mandatory full-magazine recovery duration only when the mode uses a conventional indivisible reload.
Calculate reload cycle share only from an explicitly defined active window and required reload interruption.
incremental_recovery: Preserve reload start time, per-unit recovery time, reload end time, and interruptibility separately when supplied.
Do not treat full-magazine refill duration as one unavoidable interruption when partial recovery permits earlier reuse.
regenerative_recovery: Preserve regeneration delay, empty-magazine delay, and recovery rate as separate properties.
Calculate partial and full recovery times from the supplied regeneration model rather than conventional reload time.
Do not assume that regeneration begins only after complete depletion.
staged_recovery: Preserve each mandatory, repeatable, and interruptible recovery phase separately.
Do not replace a staged profile with one aggregate duration unless the source explicitly defines that value as the complete unavoidable interruption.
empty_magazine_penalty: Use the empty-magazine delay or recovery behavior only after complete depletion.
Do not apply the empty-state penalty to partial recovery cycles.
variable_consumption_mode: Do not derive one fixed magazine duration when ammunition cost or event frequency changes across attack phases or conditions.
partial_magazine_use: Treat the full-depletion cycle as a standardized upper-use scenario rather than guaranteed player behavior.
Do not report maximum reload-event frequency as practical reload frequency.

exceptions: Do not classify reload friction from reload time, magazine size, or recovery model alone.
Do not use displayed fire rate as magazine-consumption rate without a validated relationship.
Do not apply conventional full-magazine logic to incremental, regenerative, or staged recovery.
Do not assume that multishot consumes additional magazine ammunition.
Do not merge reload behavior across attack modes.
Do not compare reload cycle share across incompatible recovery models without preserving their timing differences.
